script: Today, we will learn about Newton's Laws of Motion. There are three important laws: the First Law, the Second Law, and the Third Law. Each one explains how objects move and interact with forces.
Newton's First Law, also called the Law of Inertia, tells us that an object at rest will stay at rest unless acted upon by an external force.
Similarly, an object already in motion will continue moving at a constant velocity, meaning it keeps the same speed and direction unless a force changes it.
When an external force pushes on a moving object, it changes the object's velocity. This means the object can speed up, slow down, or change direction depending on the force applied.
Newton's Second Law explains how force, mass, and acceleration are related. The formula F equals m times a shows that force equals mass multiplied by acceleration.
If we apply the same force to two objects with different masses, the lighter object will accelerate faster, while the heavier object accelerates more slowly.
Newton's Third Law states that for every action force, there is an equal and opposite reaction force. This means forces always come in pairs, acting on two different objects.
When two objects push against each other, they exert equal and opposite forces, causing both to move slightly in opposite directions.
